page: Google Cloud Platform a4x-maxgpu-4g-metal is an accelerator-optimized bare-metal server configured with four NVIDIA GB300 GPUs. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 144 dedicated vCPUs and 960.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 6.67 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance type, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a resource density perspective, this server represents a high-end hardware class designed to maximize parallel processing capabilities. The absence of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider when planning deployments. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without virtualization overhead, aligning with high-density compute workloads.

This hardware profile is engineered for workloads requiring massive parallel computation and high memory capacity. Suitable applications include large-scale machine learning model training, deep learning inference, high-performance computing simulations, and complex data science pipelines that leverage multi-GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform a4x-maxgpu-4g-metal is an accelerator-optimized bare-metal server designed for high-density parallel computing. It features 144 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, 960.0 GB of system memory, and four integrated NVIDIA GB300 GPUs. The system provides 6.67 GB of RAM per core and does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile is optimized for workloads that require heavy GPU acceleration and substantial memory capacity, such as machine learning training, deep learning inference, and high-performance computing simulations. The lack of local storage requires external storage solutions, representing a key architectural tradeoff for deployment planning.
